mnemonics: My Dog Is Very Smart - M.D.I.V.S. stands for the four properties of a metric (d): M (Non-negativity), D (Definiteness), I (Symmetry), V (Triangle Inequality), S (Spaces)
Please Don't Ignore Very Tricky Subjects - to recall the four metric space axioms: Positive definiteness, Definiteness, Identity of indiscernibles, Value symmetry, Triangle inequality, Separation
keyPoints: A metric space is a set X with a distance function d: X × X → ℝ satisfying: 1) d(x,y) ≥ 0, 2) d(x,y) = 0 iff x = y, 3) d(x,y) = d(y,x), 4) d(x,z) ≤ d(x,y) + d(y,z)
Common examples include: ℝⁿ with Euclidean distance, any set with discrete metric (0 if x=y, 1 otherwise), function spaces with sup norm
The metric defines open balls, which generate a topology on X, making metric spaces a special class of topological spaces
commonMistakes: Assuming all sets are metric spaces - some sets cannot be equipped with a valid metric function
Forgetting to verify the triangle inequality when proving a function is a metric - it's often the trickiest property to check
Confusing metric spaces with normed spaces - all normed spaces are metric spaces, but not all metric spaces come from norms
